title: Architecture
content:
Architecture of a system describes its major components, its relationships and how they interact with each other. Software architecture is the high-level, strategic blueprint of a system, defining its overall structure, core components, and their interactions to meet business needs, while design is the tactical, low-level process of detailing how individual components will be built and work together. Think of architecture as defining the what and why (using a microservices pattern for scalability), whereas design is the how of specific implementation, like choosing a particular design pattern (Strategy pattern) to solve a problem within one of those services.

Key decisions:
  • Choosing an overall pattern like microservices or serverless.
  • Addressing non-functional requirements like security, performance, and scalability across the system.
  • Defining the interactions between major components and systems.

title: Reactive
content:
Reactive programming is a declarative programming paradigm concerned with data streams and the propagation of change. Reactive programming allows applications to react to updates automatically and efficiently. Like reacting to something when user clicks this button run this function or when file IO operation is complete run this function. Imperative means when we are doing all the steps, while declarative is where we are declaring this is what i want to happen and something is going to make that happen. An imperative approach involves explicitly writing out the sequence of steps to achieve a result, while a declarative approach describes the desired outcome and the programming language figures out the execution. Main purpose of reactive programming is to avoid blocking in our code.

Synchronous = Sequential
Asynchronous = Non-sequential

List<Person> findByName(String name) -> void findByName(String name, Callback<List<Person>> persons)
BigDecimal getIncome(String name) -> void getIncome(String name, Callback<BigDecimal> income)

title: SAGA
content:
Saga pattern is a design pattern used in microservices architecture to manage distributed transactions. This allows for a sequence of local transactions across multiple services, ensuring data consistency even when individual services might fail. It achieves this by defining a series of compensating transactions that can be executed to undo previous operations if a failure occurs. There are two main ways to implement: choreography and orchestration.

Orchestration: a central coordinator service manages the saga, instructing each service when to perform its local or compensating transaction. Choreography: services react to events published by other services, deciding whether to perform their local transaction or a compensating transaction based on the event.

Compensating transactions: for each local transaction, a corresponding compensating transaction is defined. When a transaction fails, the saga executes these compensating transactions in reverse order to undo the changes made by the successful transactions. Saga pattern relies on asynchronous communication to trigger subsequent transactions and compensating actions ensuring loose coupling between services.

In monolith app when we have a set of operations which we want to perform as a unit, we can simply use database transaction but in case of microservices based application when we have

title: ORM
content:
Object-relational mapping (ORM) is the process of mapping java objects to relational tables of a relational database. Each row in the table correspond to the single object and each column in the table correspond to the each field of the object.

ORM is an layer between object-oriented language and SQL language of relational database.
ORM layer work like a adapter layer as it adapts the language of object graphs to the language of relational tables.
Data is structured into object graphs in the object oriented program and relational tables in the relational database. JPA is the java standard API for object-relational mapping which is one of the many possible approach to ORM.

The API is a set of concepts that defines which objects within the application should be persisted and how it should persist them. JPA is only an specification and that it needs an implementation to work. Most popular implementation of JPA is Hibernate. JPA is concerned with persistence, which means any mechanism by which java objects outlive the application process that created them.

Advantages of using ORM include database independence, performance enhancements through caching, and simplified development by abstracting SQL and automating table creation.

title: Noted
content:
Stateful architecture remembers client data from one request to the next, while a stateless architecture does not. CQRS pattern help us scale read and write operations independently. Search tree have the initial state at the root. Branches of search tree are actions. Search tree nodes corresponds to states in the state space of the problem. Search tree have repeated states: such repeated states may make the search tree infinite. Latency measured in milliseconds (ms) represents the time taken for a single query to execute. Latency is the time it takes to process one request; throughput is how many requests are handled per second. Throughput measured in operations per second or transactions per second represents the volume of data processed per unit time.
Latency focuses on the speed of each operations while throughput focuses on the volume of operations that can be handled per unit time.
Data structure is a way of organizing data so that it can be used (updated, queried and deleted) effectively.
Heartbeat is a small, regular signal sent between components to check if they’re still alive and responsive.
SPOF is a component in our system whose failure can bring down the entire system down.
Authentication verifies the identity of a user or service and authorization determines their access rights.
Authentication is the process of verifying a user's identity while authorization is the process of determining what actions a user can perform.
HTTP keep-alive or persistent connection feature allows a single TCP connection to be reused for multiple HTTP requests and responses.
A rate limiter controls how many requests a client can make within a specific timeframe.
Library is a tool that provides a specific functionality.
Framework is a set of tools and guidelines for building the application.
Transaction: a series of database operations that either all succeed or they all fail.
Normalization is technique of organizing the data into multiple related tables to minimize data redundancy.
Primary key: candidate key chosen to uniquely identify each row of data in a table.
Foreign key: an attribute in table which is used to define its relationship with another table.
DAO is a design pattern to implement persistent layer of the Java application and reduces coupling between business logic and persistent logic.
Pluggable code is a system architecture where components can be easily added, removed or replaced without changing existing code.
Singleton: client-server model where client class can have many objects but server class must have a single object.

title: Cache
DNS
content:
DNS is a hierarchical and distributed name service that provides a naming system for computers, services and other resources on the internet or other Internet Protocol (IP) networks.

GeoDNS (Geographical Domain Name System) is a DNS service that redirects users to different servers based on their geographical location, optimizing traffic flow for better performance and availability. It works by identifying a user's IP address, determining their geographic location using IP geolocation databases, and then returning the IP address of a server that is closest or most appropriate for that region.

title: API-Gateway
content: API gateway acts as a single entry point for handling client requests to various backend services. It acts as a reverse proxy, routing requests to the appropriate services and can also handle cross-cutting concerns like authentication, authorization, rate limiting, caching, logging, monitoring, protocol translation and load balancing. API gateway accepts API requests from a client, processes them based on defined policies, directs them to the appropriate services, and combines the responses for a simplified user experience.

title: Load-balancer
content: Load balancing is the method of distributing network traffic equally across a pool of resources that support an application. Modern applications must process millions of users simultaneously and return the correct text, videos, images, and other data to each user in a fast and reliable manner. To handle such high volumes of traffic, most applications have many resource servers with duplicate data between them. A load balancer is a device that sits between the user and the server group and acts as an invisible facilitator, ensuring that all resource servers are used equally. Companies usually have their application running on multiple servers. Such a server arrangement is called a server farm. User requests to the application first go to the load balancer. The load balancer then routes each request to a single server in the server farm best suited to handle the request. Benefits of load balancer: load balancer improves an application’s availability, scalability, security and performance. We can use load balancing to make these tasks easier: Run application server maintenance or upgrades without application downtime. Load-balancer allows us to roll out software updates without taking the whole service down.

title: Rate-limiter
content: Rate limiting is the technique for limiting the number of request that can be made to a web service in a given period of time. For monetising an API rate limit is very important when you want to charge your client based on number of request made.

title: API
content: API is a set of rules and protocols that allows different software applications to communicate with each other. API authentication ensures that right person is accessing the right data. A web service helps us expose the functionality or data of an application to another applications over world wide web. Web services provide a web-based interface that makes the services of an application available to other applications on the web.

title: Cookie
content: Cookie data is stored on the client’s computer. Cookies can be set to expire after a specific time period or when the browser is closed. In some cases, it is essential that all requests from a client are sent to the same server for the duration of a session, for example when a client is putting items in a shopping cart and then completing the purchase. Maintaining the connection between client and server is known as session persistence. Without session persistence, data has to be synchronized across servers and potentially fetched multiple times which results in performance loss. Session data is stored on the server.

title: Distributed
content:
Distributed computing involves multiple computers working together over a network to solve a problem, acting as a single, powerful system. It's a divide-and-conquer approach where tasks are broken down and processed concurrently on different nodes (computers), then the results are combined. This allows for greater scalability, performance, and resilience compared to using a single, centralized computer. The components of a distributed system communicate and coordinate their actions by passing messages to one another in order to achieve a common goal. Three significant challenges of distributed systems are: maintaining concurrency of components, overcoming the lack of a global clock, and managing the independent failure of components. When a component of one system fails, the entire system does not fail. Examples of distributed systems vary from SOA-based systems to microservices to massively multiplayer online games to peer-to-peer applications. Distributed systems cost significantly more than monolithic architectures, primarily due to increased needs for additional hardware, servers, gateways, firewalls, new subnets, proxies, and so on. There are many different types of implementations for the message passing mechanism, including pure HTTP, RPC connectors and message queues.

System design is the process of defining the architecture, components, interfaces and data of a software system to meet specific requirements. System design is a crucial process that ensures a system is well-structured, efficient, and can meet the evolving needs of its users.

Key Concepts in Distributed System Design:
  • Nodes or components: These are the individual computers or processes that make up the distributed system.
  • Network: A network connects these nodes, enabling communication and data exchange.
  • Communication: Many protocols and mechanisms (REST, RPC, MQ) are used for communication between nodes.
  • Transparency: Users should not be aware of the underlying complexity of the distributed nature of the system.
  • Scalability: The system should be able to handle increasing load and data volume by adding more nodes.
  • Availability: The system should remain operational and accessible even if some nodes fail.
  • Consistency: Ensuring data consistency across multiple nodes is a significant challenge.
  • Fault Tolerance: The system should be designed to handle failures gracefully and continue functioning.
  • Security: How secure should the system be? Consider factors like authentication, encryption, and access controls.
  • Durability: How durable should the system be? What is the timeframe for data retention.

title: Hashing
content:
Hashing is the process of converting an input of any size into a fixed-size string of characters using a hash function. A hash function is a mathematical process that converts a variable-length input into a fixed-length output called a hash or digest. These functions are one-way and collision-resistant, meaning they are easy to compute one way but very difficult to reverse, and different inputs should produce different outputs. Hash function must be also deterministic. They are widely used for data integrity checks, secure password storage, and authentication in applications like cryptography, blockchain, and data structures.

Consistent hashing is a hashing technique that minimizes the number of keys that need to be remapped when a hash table is resized, useful in distributed systems. Consistent hashing is a distributed hashing algorithm designed to address the problem of data redistribution when nodes are added or removed. With traditional hashing algorithms adding or removing node often result in alot of data needing to be distributed which can lead to system instability and performance degradation. Goal of consistent hashing is to minimize the need for rehashing when the number of nodes in a system changes. Consistent hashing is used in distributed systems, such as web caches, CDNs and DBs to efficiently distribute data or load across a variable number of servers or nodes.

title: Scalability
content: Scalability is the ability of a system to handle an increased workload without loosing performance. The ability of a system to handle a growing amount of work by adding resources. The ability of a system to handle increasing traffic, data, or users without breaking or slowing down.

title: Client-server
content: The client-server model is a computing model where client devices request resources or services from a centralized server, which then processes the request and provides a response. Client-server model also called request-response model in which client sends a request and has to wait for the server's response before sending another one. Client-server model does not support continuos flow (live streaming) of data. For update client has to ask the server, server itself does not provides realtime updates.

title: EDA
content: Event-driven architecture is a software design pattern that allows system to detect, process and respond to events. A system design paradigm where components react to events rather than relying on synchronous request-response patterns

title: Shard
content: Sharding is the process of dividing a database table (dataset) into smaller, more manageable pieces called shards, which are distributed across multiple machines or nodes. Sharding usually refers to splitting one large table’s data horizontally (row-wise) across multiple databases or servers. Each shard contains a subset of rows of the table. All shards have the same schema, just different data. Splits large datasets into smaller chunks (shards) to spread the load across servers.

title: Connections
content:
Database connection pooling is a way to reduce the cost of opening and closing connections by maintaining a pool of open connections that can be passed from one database operation to another database operation as needed. This way, we are spared the expense of having to open and close a brand new connection for each operation the database is asked to perform. Connection pooling improves the performance of the application by reducing the latency associated with establishing new database connections, leading to faster query execution and overall application response times. A connection pooler is responsible for opening, closing and maintaining connections to the database on behalf of clients.

For a client application to open a connection to a database, a surprising number of steps must occur:
  • Any DNS lookups required to locate the IP address of the database server
  • Conduct the three-way handshake required to establish a TCP connection to the server
  • Negotiate and enable encryption for the connection through a TLS handshake
  • Exchange preferences and requirements with the database software to establish the session parameters
  • Perform database authentication checks to establish the client's identity
  • Perform initial authorization checks to establish that the client has access to the requested database objects
  • Perform the actual query and return the results
  • Tear down the database session, TLS encryption, and TCP connection

title: Communication
content:
Communication between two services (A, B) is synchronous or blocking when service A call service B and service A will keep waiting till the service B returns the response.

An event is any type of action, incident, or change that's identified or recorded by software or applications. Topic is basic unit for categorizing data in kafka. Kafka stream API can be used to transform or aggregate topics before reaching to consumers.

Message queue allows asynchoronous communication between microservices.
Message queues enables asynchronous communication between different parts of a system.

title: ACID
content:
ACID properties define the reliability and integrity of database transactions. They ensure that transactions are processed reliably and that the database remains in a consistent state, even in the face of system failures.

Transactions are treated as a single, indivisible unit of work.
Either all operations within a transaction are completed successfully, or none of them are.
If any part of the transaction fails, the entire transaction is rolled back to preventing partial updates.
A transaction must bring the database from one valid state to another.
This means that any transaction must respect all defined constraints, rules and restrictions of the database.
Concurrent transactions should not interfere with each other.
Each transaction should be isolated from others, as if they were running sequentially.
This prevents issues like dirty reads (reading uncommitted data) or lost updates.
Once a transaction is committed, its changes are permanent and survive system failures.
The database must guarantee that the data will be written to persistent storage and will not be lost due to power outages or other crashes.

title: Java
content:
Objects do not create other objects on which they relay to do their work, instead they get the objects that they need from an outside world.
Abstraction is separation of interface from implementation so that we can have one interface and many implementations or usages. Abstract data type: what operations can be performed on this data, ignoring how those operations will be performed. High level modules should not depend on low level modules rather both should depends on abstraction. Polymorphism is the ability of a variable, function or object to take on multiple forms. Interface defines what a class does but not how it does it. Advantages of generics: compile-time type safety, avoids class cast exceptions and eliminate the need for most type conversions. Try-catch promotes separation of concern principles by avoiding mixing of business logic code and error handling code. Volatile ensures that all the threads sees the same value of a variale at any given time. There is one-to-one mapping between JVM platform threads and OS threads. Cached shared data causes a thread not to notice that data has been changed by the another thread. Returned value is wrapped into the Future object, which is returned immediately. Method get blocks invoking thread until timeout or when the value within the Future object becomes available. Method wait puts a thread into waiting state against specific monitor. Any number of threads can be waiting against the same monitor. Keyword synchronized enforces exclusive access to the block of code. Creating platform threads is expensive both in terms of time and space. Thread does not have to block on anthing for the final result of the pipeline to be relevant.
Use IntStream instead of Stream<Integer> when possible.
Interrupts are co-operation mechanism for one thread to tell other thread to stop its execution.

title: Thread
content:
Thread is a dispatchable unit of work, includes a processor context (program counter and stack pointer) and its own data area for a stack. Each thread is executing the same program code and shares the same data area and heap however each thread has it own stack containing local variables.

Cached shared data causes a thread not to notice that data has been changed by the another thread.

title: Optimal
content:
Cache results of the queries for fast read operation which reduces load on the database.
Use database indexing to improve read operations.
Use flyweight design pattern to cache shared object states.
Use eager loading in hibernate instead of lazy loading.
When threads becomes non-blocking, your task is waiting but threads should take on next one.
Distributed transaction span across the multiple microservices.
Use interfaces for behavior and abstract classes for shared logic.
Prefer composition over inheritance where possible.
Always discuss scalability, concurrency, and thread safety.
Structure your solution into clear layers like controller, service, repository, and model.
Apply SOLID and DRY principles naturally in your reasoning.
Handle failures and exceptions gracefully.
Use join fetch or eager loading in hibernate.
Create indexes on highly selected columns.

title: Microservices
content:
Microservice architecture is an architectural pattern that organizes an application into a collection of small, independent and loosely coupled services. Each service focuses on a specific business capability, can be developed, deployed and scaled independently and communicates with others through lightweight protocols like APIs. Main characteristics of microservice architecture is the ability to develop, deploy, operate and scale each module independently. These services are loosely coupled, independently deployable and often developed and scaled separately, enabling greater flexibility and agility in managing complex systems. Each component service in a microservices architecture can be developed, deployed, operated and scaled without affecting the functioning of other services. Services do not need to share any of their code or implementation with other services. Any communication between individual components happens via well-defined APIs.

Advantages:
  • Agility: Faster delivery cycles, as teams can work and deploy services independently.
  • Scalability: The ability to scale individual services as needed is more efficient than scaling a monolith.
  • Resilience: Failure in one service is isolated, improving overall application reliability.
  • Team autonomy: Small teams can own and manage specific services, which promotes greater agility.
Challenges:
  • Complexity: Managing a distributed system can be more complex than a single monolithic application.
  • Communication overhead: Network latency and handling errors in service-to-service communication can be challenging.
  • Data consistency: Ensuring data consistency across multiple, independent services can be difficult.

title: Monolith
content:
Monolithic architecture is a software design pattern where all components of an application like the user interface, business logic and data access layer are built and deployed as a single, unified unit.

Disadvantages:
  • Tight coupling - modules are inter-dependent meaning changes in one module result in breaking of other module.
  • Failure in one module can brings down the entire application.
  • Any change requires re-deploying the whole system, even for small updates.

title: Database
content:
Data is the digital representation of the information that can be stored on the electronic devices and transmitted over network.
Date can be in form of numbers, text, images, videos, sounds etc.
A database is a collection of data modeled and organized in a specific way.
Software that manages databases are called database management system (DBMS).
Depending on how data is modeled and organized, database is grouped into two categories (SQL, NoSQL).
Data (Relational DB) = Structured into set of tables and set of relationships between tables.
Foreign key is what makes relational database relational.

A primary key is a column or set of columns in a database table that uniquely identifies each row.
  • Uniqueness: Each value in the primary key column(s) must be unique, so no two rows have the same primary key.
  • Non-null: A primary key column cannot have a null value because a null value cannot be used to uniquely identify a row.
  • Automatic indexing: Most database systems automatically create a unique index on the primary key to speed up data retrieval.
  • Single or composite: Can be a single col or a combination of multiple cols (composite key) that together, uniquely identify each row.
A foreign key is a column in one table, known as the child table, that references the primary key (or a unique key) of another table.
A foreign key constraint is a rule in relational databases that establishes and enforces a link between two tables.
A foreign key ensures referential integrity, meaning that relationships between tables remain consistent and valid.
The primary purpose of a foreign key is to maintain referential integrity. This means that every value in the foreign key column(s) of the child table must either exist as a value in the referenced primary key column of the parent table, or be NULL (if the foreign key column is nullable). This prevents actions that would create orphan records in the child table, where a record refers to a non-existent record in the parent table.

title: DI
content: Dependency injection is a programming technique where an object or function receives its dependencies (other objects or functions it needs) from an external source, rather than creating them itself. This approach, which is a form of Inversion of Control, makes code more flexible, maintainable, and testable by decoupling components and allowing them to be replaced or mocked easily.

title: Remember
content:
Consider writing our entire application using log4j API. One day we decide to switch back to logback then we will have to change all the classes that uses log4j API.

title: Indexing
content:
Database indexing is a technique that improves the speed of data retrieval by creating a data structure, like a lookup table, that points to the data's location. When you create an index on a table column, a separate data structure is built with the values from that column and pointers to the corresponding rows in the main table.

A clustered index physically sorts and stores a table's data based on its key values, like a dictionary, while a non-clustered index is a separate structure that contains the index keys and pointers to the data rows. This means a table can have only one clustered index but can have multiple non-clustered indexes. Clustered indexes are faster for data retrieval because the data is stored in the same order as the index, but they can increase overhead for data modification operations (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE). Non-clustered indexes are slower to query but more flexible, as they don't affect the physical order of the table data.

title: Pools
content:
A thread pool is a collection of pre-created worker threads that are kept ready to perform tasks, avoiding the overhead of creating and destroying new threads for each task. When a task is submitted, it is assigned to an available thread from the pool. If all threads are busy, the task waits in a queue until a thread becomes free. Thread pools improve performance by reusing threads and help manage resources by limiting the number of threads running concurrently.

How it works:
  • Task submission: Tasks are submitted to the thread pool, not directly to individual threads.
  • Task assignment: An available worker thread from the pool is assigned to the task.
  • Queueing: If all threads are busy, the task is placed in a waiting queue.
  • Thread reuse: After completing a task, the thread returns to the pool to wait for another task, instead of being terminated.
Benefits:
  • Improved performance: Reduces the significant cost of creating and destroying threads for each task.
  • Simplified programming: Developers can focus on the task logic rather than the complexities of managing thread lifecycles.
  • Resource management: Limits the total number of threads, preventing the system from running out of resources like memory.
Key characteristics:
  • Worker threads: A set of threads that are created at the start and are always ready to execute tasks.
  • Task queue: A queue to hold tasks when all threads in the pool are busy.
  • Configurable: Thread pools allow configuration of the number of threads, type of queue to use and how to handle rejected tasks.
